rulings.Case Title: EUGENE PITT SHEFFIELD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For An Order Granting Relief From The Courts Assessment Of A First Paper Filing Fee To Each Intervenor; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Reduced To A Total Of $410.00 For All Intervenors; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Equitably Reduced;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ motion for relief from assessment of a first paper filing fee is granted. Intervenors are not required to pay any first filing fee as they had already paid such fee on behalf of Scott Co., their suspended insured. The $2050.00 assessed against them is vacated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Granting Relief From The Courts Assessment Of A First Paper Filing Fee To Each Intervenor; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Reduced To A Total Of $410.00 For All Intervenors; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Equitably Reduced;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Intervenors¿ motion for relief from assessment of a first paper filing fee is granted. Intervenors are not required to pay any first filing fee as they had already paid such fee on behalf of Scott Co., their suspended insured. The $2050.00 assessed against them is vacated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RALPH WARNER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary judgment is denied as to issue 1 (loss of consortium) and off calendar as to issue 2 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment, Mr. Warner¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition testimony of Mr. DeMarse create a triable issue whether Mr. Warner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Goodyearite sheet gasket material) attributable to defendant. Summary adjudication of Mrs. Warner¿s cause of action for loss of consortium is denied because Mr. and Mrs. Warner were married at the time of diagnosis. (Vanhooser v. Superior Court (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 921.) Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Warner¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PERNIZ VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Granting Relief From The Courts Assessment Of A First Paper Filing Fee To Each Intervenor; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Reduced To A Total Of $410.00 For All Intervenors; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed First Paper Filing Fees Be Equitably Reduced
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 27. Intervenors¿ motion that the first paper filing fees be reduced to a total of $410.00 for all Intervenors is granted. The $1640.00 difference between the $2050.00 they were initially assessed and the $410.00 they must pay is vacated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
